examples.code:
# Sorting complex nested structure with multi-key lambda
users = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'role': 'user', 'age': 25},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'role': 'admin', 'age': 30},
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'role': 'user', 'age': 20}
]
users.sort(key=lambda u: (u['role'], -u['age']))
print(users)
examples.explanation: Returns a tuple `(role, -age)` to perform multi-level composite sorting.
